analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS on treatment_tamoxifen=4.723 (n=15035) vs off=4.680 (n=34965); diff=+0.043 months, t=1.77, p=0.077. Palbociclib stands out (large positive); pembrolizumab and trastuzumab show small negative associations likely from confounding by indication.

Mean PFS on treatment_palbociclib=5.408 (n=17428) vs off=4.310 (n=32572); diff=+1.098 months, t=43.54, p<1e-300. Palbociclib stands out (large positive); pembrolizumab and trastuzumab show small negative associations likely from confounding by indication.

Mean PFS on treatment_trastuzumab=4.645 (n=10039) vs off=4.705 (n=39961); diff=-0.060 months, t=-2.18, p=0.029. Palbociclib stands out (large positive); pembrolizumab and trastuzumab show small negative associations likely from confounding by indication.

Mean PFS on treatment_olaparib=4.660 (n=4946) vs off=4.696 (n=45054); diff=-0.036 months, t=-0.97, p=0.330. Palbociclib stands out (large positive); pembrolizumab and trastuzumab show small negative associations likely from confounding by indication.

Mean PFS on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=4.686 (n=4987) vs off=4.694 (n=45013); diff=-0.007 months, t=-0.19, p=0.846. Palbociclib stands out (large positive); pembrolizumab and trastuzumab show small negative associations likely from confounding by indication.

Mean PFS on treatment_pembrolizumab=4.630 (n=7585) vs off=4.704 (n=42415); diff=-0.074 months, t=-2.38, p=0.018. Palbociclib stands out (large positive); pembrolizumab and trastuzumab show small negative associations likely from confounding by indication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on all 31 patient features and 6 treatment indicators yields an adjusted prognostic model with substantial explanatory power (R^2>0.5), and the per-feature signs will match their univariate directions for strongly prognostic features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an OLS with treatment_palbociclib, a triple-marker indicator (ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT), and their interaction predicting pfs_months, the interaction coefficient is positive and significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<median (=15.0)], treatment_palbociclib produces a substantially longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib.

In the relaxed subgroup that drop ER+ requirement (only require: HER2-, PIK3CA-WT, low_Ki67), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).

In the relaxed subgroup that drop HER2- requirement (only require: ER+, PIK3CA-WT, low_Ki67), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).

In the relaxed subgroup that drop PIK3CA-WT requirement (only require: ER+, HER2-, low_Ki67), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).

In the relaxed subgroup that drop low_Ki67 requirement (only require: ER+, HER2-, PIK3CA-WT), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).
When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when ER+=1 than when ER+=0 (each marker is necessary).
When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when HER2-=1 than when HER2-=0 (each marker is necessary).
When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when PIK3CA-WT=1 than when PIK3CA-WT=0 (each marker is necessary).
When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when low_Ki67=1 than when low_Ki67=0 (each marker is necessary).
Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is large when ki67_pct < ~14 and essentially absent when ki67_pct >= ~14 (a sharp Ki67 threshold).

FINAL: The treatment effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months is concentrated in the joint subgroup [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 (i.e., HER2-) AND pik3ca_mutation=0 (i.e., PIK3CA-WT) AND ki67_pct<14], where palbociclib lengthens mean PFS by ~+5 months versus no palbociclib. Outside this subgroup the effect of palbociclib on PFS is statistically indistinguishable from zero. Each of the 4 markers is necessary for the full effect — relaxing any single requirement materially reduces the magnitude (drop ER+ → +3.5; drop HER2- → +4.1; drop PIK3CA-WT → +3.2; drop low-Ki67 → +2.9), and the within-complement test confirms each marker is required.

FINAL: Of the six treatments studied, only treatment_palbociclib shows a robust, biomarker-defined causal-looking effect on pfs_months. After adjustment for prognostic covariates, the population-level effects of treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_olaparib, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months are all statistically indistinguishable from zero, and biomarker-defined subgroup analyses (trastuzumab in HER2+, tamoxifen in ER+, olaparib in BRCA1+/BRCA2+, sacituzumab in HER2-low / TNBC, pembrolizumab in TNBC / brain mets) fail to reveal a subgroup in which any of these treatments has a clinically meaningful, adjustment-robust PFS benefit.
